mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations rise sharply.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL stays above 250.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant across rolling 2-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO drops below the 0.5% threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during late-cycle phases.
